description: Use, control and administration of centralized and distributed data bases. Topics include the definition, design, creation, revision, interrogation, update, security and integrity of data bases.
Exploration of subject areas possibly to be introduced into the business curriculum.

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Sophomore standing or declared in undergraduate Business Exchange program

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: Sophomore standing or declared in undergraduate Business Exchange program

course_title: ANALYSIS AND DESIGN OF COMPUTER-BASED SYSTEMS
description: Analysis of business systems to identify possible need for new or improved computer-based systems and the design of systems to meet those needs.

Applied principles and techniques of information systems development including requirements identification and analysis, process and data modeling, team communication and collaboration, and system testing.

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: INFO SYS 322(422 prior to Fall 2023) andINFO SYS 371

prerequisites.prerequisites_text: INFO SYS 322(422 prior to Fall 2023) andINFO SYS 371

description: An introduction to data analysis procedures covering topics including: data collection, pre-processing, analysis, and presentation. Content covers important data analysis concepts, such as structured and unstructured data. Learn about supervised, unsupervised learning methods and advanced analytics topics, such as text mining, recommendation systems, and algorithm bias.
Focus is on developing applications and business information systems on the web using a variety of programming languages and tools. Emphasis on deployment as well as design concepts.

An introduction to text mining and natural language processing for business applications. Provides an overview of text data and steps to make it usable and approaches for making text data useful in descriptive and predictive analytics applications. Topics include representation approaches, topic modeling, and an overview of key applications of natural language processing, such as chatbots and recommender systems.

description: Focus on the economic and technical concepts behind emerging information systems. Industry cases covering platforms, online markets, artificial intelligence, and business analytics. Caters to interests in technology consulting.

Introduction to database management systems with a focus on relational databases. Covers designing, creating, populating, managing, and retrieving data from databases. SQL will be used with a focus on querying for business applications and intelligence, data retrieval for summary reports, and data visualization. Offers a brief introduction to non-relational databases for business applications.
